rationale: All AC items satisfied: storage config with three URL fields defaulting to memory://, per-trait dispatch fns in storage/dispatch.rs, server constructs backends via dispatch, unknown schemes fail fast at startup with clear error messages naming the bad scheme and supported alternatives. Test coverage includes defaults, overrides, and unknown-scheme failures for all three traits.

findings.summary: dispatch_group_coordinator takes a pre-built ConsumerGroupManager rather than constructing it from a URL+offset_store; works for memory:// but the signature will need to change once a real coordinator backend is added.

Coordinator dispatch result is bound to `_coordinator` and discarded; the manager actually used by the server is the one constructed before dispatch. Fine for memory://, but couples behavior tightly to that scheme.

Config uses individual flat fields (storage_log/offsets/groups) rather than a nested [storage] config block as described in the bead; functionally equivalent and provides a StorageConfig view via Config::storage().
